adapter_fields.finding: The physical context is real, but the code family and schedule are already present before the claimed logical appearance.
adapter_fields.value: fixed physical qubit Hilbert space, stabilizer/gauge checks, noise model, boundary conditions, and time-periodic measurement schedule

adapter_fields.finding: The admissibility predicate is supplied by the fixed schedule, gauge/stabilizer constraints, and decoder rather than generated as a new source-side law.
adapter_fields.value: logical-sector admissibility induced by the predeclared schedule and decoder

adapter_fields.finding: The candidate has a concrete code-dynamics signal and a useful negative-control shape.
adapter_fields.value: time-scheduled appearance of a logical qubit or protected subsystem

adapter_fields.finding: The witness can be preserved without granting source issuance.
adapter_fields.value: syndrome history, logical operator support, decoder transcript, and record trace

adapter_fields.finding: The successor context is a scheduled member of the fixed code family, not a source-owned new construction grammar.
adapter_fields.value: same fixed code family after one period or after decoder-visible access changes

adapter_fields.finding: Record preservation is available and is not the blocking field.

exact_missing_source_object.also_required: An internal anti-after-naming principle plus W4 perturbation non-factorization under the same preserved syndrome, logical-support, boundary, and decoder records.
exact_missing_source_object.required: A native physical source law in which the code source creates a new admissibility predicate, logical algebra, or construction grammar not representable as fixed Hilbert space, fixed stabilizer/gauge family, fixed time-periodic schedule, boundary data, decoder/readout access, noise or syndrome random tape, or completed syndrome history.

fixed_source_rival.reason: The logical-sector appearance is reproduced by one fixed physical Hilbert space and stabilizer/gauge family under a fixed time-periodic schedule, boundary data, decoder/readout rule, noise or syndrome tape, and completed syndrome history.

next_required_test: another non-overlapping physical candidate with a source-owned transition law, or a bounded no-go target for scheduled-code logical-sector candidates under fixed Hilbert-space completion

witness_gates.finding: A logical algebra appears relative to a time slice or decoder, but it is represented by the fixed physical algebra plus the predeclared stabilizer/gauge schedule.
Logical admissibility is defined by the schedule, boundary, syndrome constraints, and decoder, all of which are fixed in the rival.
The protected subsystem may be absent in an instantaneous static description and present over a period, but the construction space is the fixed circuit and measurement family.
Perturbations factor through schedule phase, boundary choice, decoder access, noise seed, gauge fixing, or completed syndrome history.
Syndrome histories, logical support, and decoder transcripts can be preserved under the frozen comparison contract.
Fixed-family, schedule, resource/access, relabeling/gauge, and completed-history absorbers are not defeated from inside the code.
